companies.name: Google
companies.tip: Opens in August for summer internships. Expect an OA followed by 2–3 virtual technical interviews. Known for structured rubric-based hiring.

companies.name: Meta
companies.tip: Recruits heavily in Aug–Oct. OA is typically on Hackerrank. Strong emphasis on data structures. Referrals move faster.

companies.name: Amazon
companies.tip: Very high volume hiring. OA is standard — two coding problems plus a work simulation survey. Leadership Principles matter in interviews.

companies.name: Apple
companies.tip: Less structured than other big tech. Positions open year-round but peak in fall. Referrals are very helpful here.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.tip: Opens in August. OA followed by a virtual loop of 2–4 interviews. Explorer program for freshmen and sophomores.

companies.name: Netflix
companies.tip: Smaller intern class, very selective. No formal OA — straight to recruiter call then technical interviews. Strong culture fit emphasis.

companies.name: Uber
companies.tip: OA on Hackerrank is common. Interviews are technical with some system design. San Francisco and NYC are main hubs.

companies.name: Airbnb
companies.tip: Smaller intern class. Strong focus on product thinking in interviews even for engineers. Values unique backgrounds.

companies.name: Stripe
companies.tip: Highly regarded engineering culture. Interviews emphasize clean code and communication. Referrals are very effective.

companies.name: Figma
companies.tip: Small intern cohort. Design and PM interviews include portfolio reviews. Strong craft and taste are valued.

companies.name: Notion
companies.tip: Small team, selective hiring. Interviews are conversational. They value strong writing skills regardless of role.

companies.name: Ramp
companies.tip: Fast-growing fintech. Values ownership and speed. Interviews move quickly — often offer within 2 weeks of final round.

companies.name: Databricks
companies.tip: Strong data engineering focus. OA is coding-based. Competitive compensation — one of the top-paying private companies.

companies.name: Snowflake
companies.tip: Opens early in August. Data and cloud focus. OA is common for engineering roles.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.tip: Unique interview format — Hackertest plus a 2-week paid work trial. Looks for product-minded engineers and analysts.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.tip: Highly competitive, small intern class. Research roles need strong ML background. Product and engineering roles are more accessible.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.tip: Research-focused culture. Strong values alignment is part of the process. Smaller cohort than peers.

companies.name: Duolingo
companies.tip: Fun culture, Pittsburgh-based. Strong emphasis on data-driven decision making for PM and engineering roles.

companies.name: Robinhood
companies.tip: OA is standard for engineering. Focus on financial product literacy in interviews.

companies.name: Spotify
companies.tip: Stockholm-based but NYC and Boston offices hire US interns. Music and data roles available. Process is structured and thorough.

companies.name: Goldman Sachs
companies.tip: Superday format with 4–6 back-to-back interviews. Apply in August — positions fill fast. Networking with alumni is essential.

companies.name: Morgan Stanley
companies.tip: Opens in July. Superday format. Technology internship is separate from investment banking — both are strong programs.

companies.name: JPMorgan
companies.tip: One of the largest intern classes on Wall Street. Apply early — positions fill in August. Good for sophomore and junior diversity programs.

companies.name: Citadel
companies.tip: OA is math and coding heavy. Extremely competitive. Quant and software engineering roles are the main tracks. Top compensation.

companies.name: Two Sigma
companies.tip: Focus on mathematical modeling and software engineering. OA includes probability and coding problems. Very strong compensation.

companies.name: Jane Street
companies.tip: Hardest OA in finance. Trading, software, and research roles. OCaml experience is a bonus. Apply early — fills in August.

companies.name: Bridgewater
companies.tip: Unique culture assessment is part of the process. Investment and technology roles. Radical transparency culture.

companies.name: Point72
companies.tip: Macro and quant analyst tracks. Rigorous process. Academy programs offer training tracks for less experienced candidates.

companies.name: BlackRock
companies.tip: Asset management giant. Analyst programs in technology, analytics, and investments. Case-style interviews for finance roles.

companies.name: McKinsey
companies.tip: Case interviews are central. Practice with BCG, McKinsey, and Bain case frameworks. Coffee chats with recruiters help. Written case for some roles.

companies.name: BCG
companies.tip: Case + fit interviews. BCG Platinion for tech consulting. Apply early in September — rounds fill quickly.

companies.name: Bain
companies.tip: STAR method for fit, case for technical. Bain is known for a collaborative culture. Written case format at some offices.

companies.name: Deloitte
companies.tip: Many tracks: audit, consulting, tax, technology. Structured interviews with behavioral and case components.

companies.name: Accenture
companies.tip: High volume recruiting. Many roles in technology and strategy. Less selective than MBB but strong global reach.

companies.name: PwC
companies.tip: Audit, tax, and advisory tracks. Leadership assessment is part of the process. Actively recruits from business and accounting programs.

companies.name: EY
companies.tip: Strong technology consulting arm (EY-Parthenon). Similar structure to PwC. Campus recruiting events are important here.

companies.name: KPMG
companies.tip: Audit and advisory tracks. Accounting and finance majors dominate. KPMG Ignite for technology roles.

companies.name: Salesforce
companies.tip: Large intern cohort. SF and NYC offices are largest. Strong sales and engineering programs. Known for good intern culture.

companies.name: Oracle
companies.tip: OA common for software roles. Cloud and database technologies dominate. Austin, TX campus is growing quickly.

companies.name: Cisco
companies.tip: Networking and security focus. San Jose headquarters. Good for networking, security, and cloud engineering roles.

companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.tip: GPU and AI chip focus. Highly technical interviews, especially for hardware and compilers. Very competitive but compensation is strong.

companies.name: Intel
companies.tip: Hardware, firmware, and software roles. Large intern class. Portland, OR and Hillsboro, OR are main sites.

companies.name: AMD
companies.tip: Strong GPU and CPU engineering roles. Competing directly with NVIDIA for AI chip talent. Austin and San Jose offices.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.tip: High volume roles across engineering disciplines. Austin, TX headquarters. Known for fast-paced culture and long hours.

companies.name: SpaceX
companies.tip: Hawthorne, CA and Boca Chica, TX. Very hands-on culture. Technical interviews emphasize first principles thinking. High bar.

companies.name: Lockheed Martin
companies.tip: Clearance roles need US citizenship. Technical and engineering focus. Stable hiring even in market downturns.

companies.name: Boeing
companies.tip: Engineering and software roles. US citizenship typically required for cleared positions. Seattle and Everett main sites.

companies.name: LinkedIn
companies.tip: Sunnyvale headquarters. Strong PM and engineering intern programs. Emphasis on economic opportunity in company values.

companies.name: TikTok
companies.tip: OA followed by 2–3 technical interviews. Mountain View office is main US hub. ByteDance parent company also hires for other products.

companies.name: Dropbox
companies.tip: Virtual-first company. Smaller intern cohort than Big Tech. Strong eng culture, known for good work-life balance.

companies.name: Lyft
companies.tip: San Francisco based. Smaller class than Uber. Interviews are technical with focus on real-world problem solving.

companies.name: DoorDash
companies.tip: Strong operations and engineering roles. Case-style interviews for ops. Engineering interviews are standard technical.

companies.name: Coinbase
companies.tip: Remote-first company. Cryptocurrency knowledge is a plus but not required. Technical interviews for engineering roles.

companies.name: Square
companies.tip: Block (parent company) includes Square, Cash App, and Tidal. San Francisco based. Multiple technical tracks available.

companies.name: Twitch
companies.tip: Amazon subsidiary. Seattle and San Francisco offices. Strong engineering culture around live streaming infrastructure.

companies.name: Zoom
companies.tip: San Jose based. Video communications and collaboration tools. Grew rapidly post-pandemic, now stabilizing headcount.

companies.name: Palo Alto Networks
companies.tip: Security-focused company. Strong for students interested in cybersecurity and cloud security.

companies.name: ServiceNow
companies.tip: Santa Clara based. Enterprise SaaS platform. Good intern program with structured rotations.

companies.name: HubSpot
companies.tip: Boston and Cambridge based. Strong marketing technology focus. Known for strong intern culture and full-time conversion.

companies.name: Atlassian
companies.tip: Distributed-first company. Sydney origins but strong US presence. Collaboration tools (Jira, Confluence) dominate enterprise.

companies.name: Slack
companies.tip: Salesforce subsidiary since 2021. San Francisco based. Engineering and PM intern programs.

companies.name: Workday
companies.tip: Pleasanton, CA based. HR and finance software platform. Good for students interested in enterprise SaaS.
